headlines.title: Three charged over Jewish charity ambulance fires
headlines.description: Four Hatzola ambulances were set alight in the car park of a synagogue in Golders Green in the early hours of 23 March.

headlines.title: Artemis II crew now halfway to Moon as they take 'spectacular' image of Earth
headlines.description: The snap was taken aboard the Orion capsule by its commander, Reid Wiseman, as the crew head towards the Moon.

headlines.title: After 16 years in power, could Viktor Orban finally be unseated?
headlines.description: Hungary is going to the polls in nine days - after 16 years in power, can Viktor Orban be unseated?

headlines.title: State pension age starts rising to 67 - here's how much you get and when
headlines.description: The age at which people can start receiving the state pension is going up in stages over the next two years.

headlines.title: Five Met Police officers taken off duty after bag of weapons is left in the street
headlines.description: A member of the public found the bag containing guns and a Taser on a south London street.

headlines.title: Football rally in Peru leaves one dead and dozens injured
headlines.description: Hundreds of Alianza Lima fans had gathered for a pregame rally ahead of a match against local rivals Universitario de Deportes.

headlines.title: How elite US teams tackle combat rescue missions
headlines.description: A former US marine tells the BBC the priority of any recovery team would be to look for signs of life.

headlines.title: Bus or Lime bike? New subscription heats up the race for a cheaper commute
headlines.description: How we travel to work in cities might be changing as e-scooter and e-bike fares become cheaper than traditional public transport.

headlines.title: Unanswered questions remain after death of Australia's most wanted fugitive
headlines.description: Double-murderer Dezi Freeman evaded capture for seven months in the bush but police believe he had help.

headlines.title: Power-washing, pool-cleaning and mowing: Why millions are playing games about mundane jobs
headlines.description: PowerWash Simulator 2 has been nominated for two Bafta Games Awards - but why have mundane job games become so popular?

headlines.title: What not to say to a friend who is struggling to conceive
headlines.description: People struggling to conceive say friends and family often make well-meaning but insensitive comments about their infertility.

headlines.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
headlines.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

headlines.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

headlines.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
headlines.description: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

headlines.title: Video appears to show US plane and search and rescue helicopters over southern Iran
headlines.description: Reports from the US, quoting unnamed officials, say a search is under way after the shooting down of a US fighter jet over Iran.

headlines.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
headlines.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

headlines.title: 'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
headlines.description: President Trump has warned Iran of strikes on bridges and electric power plants if its leaders do not agree to his terms to end the war.

headlines.title: Storm Dave set to bring Easter gales, rain and blizzards to UK
headlines.description: Damage and travel disruption are likely in the north of the UK with a Met Office named storm expected to sweep through on Saturday.

headlines.title: Russia chose 'Easter escalation' over ceasefire, says Zelensky
headlines.description: Six civilians were killed and 40 others injured as Russia launched hundreds of drones and missiles.

headlines.title: 'Iran shoots down US jet' and 'race to find pilot'
headlines.description: The downing of a US fighter jet in Iran and the search for its pilot dominates Saturday's papers.

headlines.title: Faced with new energy shock, Europe asks if reviving nuclear is the answer
headlines.description: As war drives up gas and fuel prices, Europeans turn again to the issue of energy independence.

headlines.title: Starmer's cost-of-living adviser calls for fuel duty cut extension
headlines.description: Lord Walker suggests the government should keep the 5p cut in place due to increased costs caused by war in the Middle East.

headlines.title: 'Not fit for purpose' - the secret history of a deadly phrase
headlines.description: The four word phrase - first uttered by Labour Home Secretary John Reid - has become a by-word for incompetence.

headlines.title: Watch: Artemis II's journey so far as it leaves Earth orbit... in 85 seconds
headlines.description: The crew will not land on the Moon on this current mission, though Nasa is preparing for a potential lunar landing by 2028.

headlines.title: Raye tops album charts with This Music May Contain Hope
headlines.description: It is Raye's second number one this year, after her hugely popular single Where The Hell Is My Husband.

headlines.title: Trump seeks $1.5tn for defence alongside domestic spending cuts
headlines.description: The US president's new budget would cut non-defence spending by 10%, partly by slashing domestic programmes.

headlines.title: Italy's Uffizi Galleries targeted in cyber-attack but denies security breach
headlines.description: Hackers were reported to have infiltrated IT systems - but the gallery says its works are safe.

headlines.title: Boy, 14, shot dead in Woolwich named as three teens held
headlines.description: A boy, 14, shot dead in south-east London is named as Eghosa Ogbebor, as three people are arrested.

headlines.title: M&S boss calls for more action on crime and abuse of staff
headlines.description: Thinus Keeve's comments come days after an M&S store was targeted during disorder in south London.

headlines.title: Five-hour rescue saves cat missing for 4 years
headlines.description: RSPCA, vets and firefighters free cat from tight space to discover Alfie had been  missing in 2022.

headlines.title: 'I haven't played Wordle since the day I sold it' says game's Welsh creator
headlines.description: Josh Wardle created Wordle to keep his girlfriend entertained during the Covid lockdowns.

headlines.title: 'I started running at 70 after daughter's death'
headlines.description: A 74-year-old grandfather is set to run three marathons in 14 days in memory of his daughter.

headlines.title: Highland cows moved over viral TikTok visitor surge
headlines.description: The cows have been moved from Hothfield Heathland after unheeded warnings, Kent Wildlife Trust says.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: NASA Returns To The Moon (And Beyond)
headlines.description: Nasa launch first crewed mission around the moon in 50 years.

headlines.title: Emma Stone and Ryan Gosling star in an award-winning musical
headlines.description: Exploring the joy and pain of pursuing your dreams

headlines.title: Guardiola wants Rodri to stay but says unhappy stars can go
headlines.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says unhappy players can leave the club, but he hopes midfielder Rodri will stay.

headlines.title: After more than 14 years at Atletico Madrid, what next for Simeone?
headlines.description: BBC Sport asks if Atletico Madrid could really part ways with manager Diego Simeone at the end of the season?

headlines.title: 'Shell of a fighter' - Chisora v Wilder predictions
headlines.description: Figures from the boxing world give their predictions for Saturday's heavyweight battle between Derek Chisora and Deontay Wilder.

headlines.title: How former Liverpool prospect found love for football again
headlines.description: After 18 years at Liverpool, Tyler Morton tells BBC Sport how moving to Lyon in the summer has helped him fall back in love with football.

headlines.title: Arteta defends Arsenal's international withdrawals
headlines.description: Mikel Arteta says Arsenal have '"a very good relationship" with most national teams, despite 11 of his players withdrawing from international duty.

headlines.title: Fake tan disasters & living two lives - inside the camp of a world champ
headlines.description: World boxing champion Lauren Price says the structure in her life gives her the "confidence" to win.

headlines.title: Trump sweeps Robertson aside to reach Tour Championship final
headlines.description: Judd Trump sweeps to a commanding 10-4 victory over Neil Robertson as he reaches the final of the Tour Championship in Manchester.
